one_liner: Pylon is a support/helpdesk platform offering a token-based REST API (accounts, tickets, tasks, custom fields) plus an official hosted MCP server for AI agent integration.

auth_methods.notes: Bearer token generated in Pylon dashboard (Settings > API Tokens), passed as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header. Verify via /me endpoint.
Official hosted MCP server (mcp.usepylon.com) reportedly supports OAuth 2.0 in addition to token auth, per search results.

self_serve_notes: Developers can generate API tokens directly from the Pylon dashboard without needing sales/admin approval. No explicit mention of API pricing tiers gating access to the core support/helpdesk REST API (the x402/micropayment and 'Pylon AI'/pylonapi.com pricing model appears to be a separate, differently-branded product from usepylon.com's helpdesk API and should not be conflated).

api_surface.notes: REST API at api.usepylon.com covering accounts, tickets/issues, tasks & projects, custom fields, contacts, knowledge base, etc. OpenAPI spec available.

evidence.supports: Bearer token auth generated from dashboard, verify with /me endpoint
REST API with OpenAPI spec, bearerAuth security scheme, accounts endpoints
Official hosted MCP server at mcp.usepylon.com and community implementations exist
API reference documentation confirming REST API surface

flags: Search results conflate two potentially distinct products: usepylon.com (support/helpdesk, this evaluation target) and pylonapi.com ('Pylon — The Action Layer for AI Agents', x402 micropayment model) — these may be unrelated companies sharing a name; pricing info about x402/USDC likely does not apply to usepylon.com's helpdesk API.
OAuth2 support for MCP server auth is asserted in search summary but not directly confirmed by scraped docs excerpts provided.
